front: I don't want the door to close on me.
back: No quiero que la puerta se me cierre.
hint: no quiero que → mood · se me = on me

front: I don't want the door to close on me again.
back: No quiero que la puerta se me cierre otra vez.

front: I don't want it to close on me.
back: No quiero que se me cierre.

front: I don't want it to happen again. (pasar)
back: No quiero que vuelva a pasar.

front: I don't want it to happen again. (ocurrir)
back: No quiero que vuelva a ocurrir.

front: I wanted them to eat with us.
I was hoping he would call me.
hint: would-call → past mood, no extra word

front: We needed to speak with him.
hint: no que → no mood · plain infinitive

front: We needed him to speak with us.
I wanted you to call me.
I was hoping you would eat with us.
We needed you to call us.
I called you again to organize.
I called you again so we could organize.
back: Volví a llamarte para que organizáramos.

front: It's not very relevant that they found it.
back: No es muy relevante que lo encontraran.

front: It's not very relevant that they (might) find it.
back: No es muy relevante que lo encuentren.
hint: present mood — same trigger, time shifts
from creer → creíble → in-creíble · accent on í

front: The incredible thing is that they come back.
hint: lo + adj + es que → no mood

front: The incredible thing is that they do it again.
back: Lo increíble es que vuelven a hacerlo.
hint: dodge mood with lo + adj + es que

front: The important thing is that he speaks with us.
back: Lo importante es que habla con nosotros.
hint: dodge — no mood after es que

front: That you bought it is not my problem.
back: Que lo compraras no es mi problema.

front: That you told him is not my problem.
back: Que le contaras no es mi problema.

front: If I could cook, I would cook.
If I could cancel it, I would cancel it.
If I could see it, I would understand it. (comprender)
If you had told me it, I would have gone.
back: Si me lo hubieras contado, habría ido.

front: If we had known, we would have gone.
If you had known, you would have gone.

back: Hablase o hablara, es lo mismo.

front: I needed you to call me again.
back: Necesitaba que me volvieras a llamar.
